data.title: Proposal for Amnesty Policy for Russian Cryptocurrency Miners Using Illegal Equipment
data.description:
According to PANews, a proposal has been made by Oleg Ogienko, a member of the Russian State Duma's cryptocurrency regulation working group, at the Digital Almaz forum. The proposal suggests implementing an amnesty policy for cryptocurrency miners using illegally imported equipment. This initiative aims to transition approximately 60% of the country's underground miners to legal operations.

Ogienko highlighted that although Russia has legalized mining this year, the absence of amnesty provisions for illegally imported equipment hinders miners from registering with the Federal Tax Service. Currently, only about 150 mining farms and 1,300 mining entities are registered nationwide. Regions with abundant electricity, such as Siberia, are experiencing power shortages due to the concentration of mining farms, leading to mining bans in over ten regions this year.

Russian law enforcement and power companies are conducting nationwide raids, using technology to locate illegal mining operations. The proposed amnesty aims to integrate underground miners into the regulatory framework, alleviate enforcement pressures, and increase tax revenue.

data.title: Interpol Identifies Crypto Fraud as Major Transnational Crime Threat
data.description:
According to PANews, Interpol has passed a resolution at its General Assembly in Marrakech, recognizing crypto-related fraud as a central component of a vast scam industry and categorizing it as a transnational crime threat. Global law enforcement agencies are intensifying coordination to manage the flow of funds within these networks. These criminal organizations employ methods such as human trafficking, cyber scams, and forced labor, affecting victims from over sixty countries. Criminals often lure victims with promises of high-paying overseas jobs, only to trap them in illegal operations involving voice phishing, romance scams, investment fraud, and cryptocurrency scams.

Scam groups recruit victims through fake job offers, transporting them to illegal sites where they are coerced into criminal activities. They utilize advanced technology to deceive victims and conceal their operations, with these cross-border crime networks showing high adaptability. Initially drawing international attention in Southeast Asia, the scam center model has now spread to parts of Russia, Colombia, East African coastal nations, and regions in the United Kingdom.

In May, the U.S. Treasury severed financial ties with Cambodia's Hui Wang Group, accusing it of laundering $4 billion for scams. This month, the U.S. Department of Justice has formed a special task force in collaboration with multiple agencies. Interpol's resolution marks a new phase in global law enforcement collaboration.

data.title: The crypto fear and greed index rose to 25, indicating the market remains in a state of "extreme fear."
data.description: Alternative data shows that today's cryptocurrency fear and greed index is 25, a significant increase from 14 last week, but market sentiment remains in the "extreme fear" range. Note: The fear index ranges from 0-100 and includes the following indicators: volatility (25%) + market volume (25%) + social media buzz (15%) + market surveys (15%) + Bitcoin's share of the overall market (10%) + Google trending topics (10%).

data.title: A whale that hoarded WBTC and ETH at high prices through revolving loans has sold off 350 WBTC at a loss.
data.description: Chain analyst Yu Jin published an article on the X platform stating that after a whale who hoarded WBTC and ETH at high prices through a revolving loan scheme sold off 18,517 ETH at a loss, incurring a loss of 25.29 million USDT, he is also gradually selling off WBTC at a loss. Over the past week, he sold 350 WBTC at an average price of $87,732, exchanging them for 30.7 million USDT. His WBTC cost price was $116,762, resulting in a loss of $10.16 million from this sale. He currently still holds 1,210 WBTC, with a paper loss of $30.9 million.

data.title: UK Financial Conduct Authority Introduces Stablecoin Project Group
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, the UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) has announced the launch of a stablecoin project group as part of its regulatory sandbox initiative, with applications open until January 18, 2026.

Additionally, Bloomberg reports that the UK Debt Management Office is considering expanding the UK government bond market, a move related to stablecoin reserves. In a recent speech, David Geale from the FCA revealed that a 'large enterprise' has been included in the sandbox program to test a pound sterling stablecoin for payments. Participants in the sandbox must ensure their designs comply with the requirements outlined in the FCA's consultation paper from May.

The UK employs a dual-track approach to stablecoin regulation: the Bank of England oversees systemically important stablecoins for prudential and financial stability purposes, while the FCA is responsible for the regulation of other stablecoins, including conduct and consumer protection.

data.title: Dogecoin and Shiba Inu fight through volatility as ETF momentum and new listings fuel hopes for a stronger year ahead
data.description:
Dogecoin and Shiba Inu, two popular cryptocurrencies that have captured the attention of investors and the general public alike, are navigating through significant volatility in the market. This tumultuous environment is driven by various factors, including the rising momentum of exchange-traded funds (ETFs) that focus on digital assets, as well as the excitement surrounding new cryptocurrency listings. These developments have sparked renewed optimism among traders and enthusiasts, fostering hopes that the upcoming year may offer improved stability and growth for both Dogecoin and Shiba Inu. As they continue to contend with price fluctuations, the potential for a more robust market performance looms on the horizon, encouraging supporters of these cryptocurrencies to remain hopeful for what lies ahead.
source: https://blocknews.com/dogecoin-and-shiba-inu-fight-through-volatility-as-etf-momentum-and-new-listings-fuel-hopes-for-a-stronger-year-ahead/

data.title: Bitwise Updates Avalanche ETF Application with Competitive Fee Structure
data.description:
According to PANews, Bitwise has updated its application for a spot Avalanche ETF with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). The revised application changes the ETF's ticker to BAVA and sets the sponsorship fee rate at 0.34%, the lowest among similar products. In comparison, VanEck's Avalanche ETF charges a fee of 0.40%, while Grayscale's is 0.50%.

The updated S-1 filing also indicates that the trust will be allowed to stake up to 70% of its AVAX holdings on Avalanche's proof-of-stake network to earn additional tokens. However, the issuer plans to deduct 12% of the staking rewards as fees, with the remainder distributed to shareholders. Currently, competitors have not engaged in staking activities, limiting their fees to sponsorship charges.

Bitwise is also offering a full fee waiver for the first $500 million in assets during the initial month, aiming to position BAVA as the most cost-effective option for traditional investors seeking exposure to Avalanche and staking income.

data.title: Conor McGregor and Khabib’s UFC Rivalry Erupts Again After NFT ‘Scam’ Accusation
data.description:
Conor McGregor and Khabib Nurmagomedov’s rivalry has returned to the spotlight, this time dominating Crypto Twitter after McGregor accused Khabib’s new Telegram-based NFT collection of scamming fans. The claim triggered a swift response from Khabib and a sharp intervention from on-chain investigator ZachXBT, who redirected attention toward McGregor’s own controversial token launch. Crypto Feud Ignites After Khabib’s NFT Launch Khabib promoted a new digital collectibles drop on Telegram this week, themed around the Dagestani papakha hat he wore during UFC walkouts. The collection sold out quickly, generating about $4
source: https://beincrypto.com/khabib-mcgregor-nft-feud-explained/

data.title: BNB Jumps 4% Despite Weak On-Chain Activity — Here Is What’s Really Driving the Move
data.description:
"BNB, the native cryptocurrency of the Binance exchange, experienced a significant increase of 4% in its value, even though the on-chain activity associated with it has shown signs of weakness. This unexpected rise prompts a closer examination of the underlying factors that are actually contributing to this upward movement, providing a deeper understanding of the current market dynamics at play."
source: https://blocknews.com/bnb-jumps-4-despite-weak-on-chain-activity-here-is-whats-really-driving-the-move/
